compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
The review-bot's structured operational logs (the `_emit()` INFO events —
`voter_voted`, `voter_skip`, `merge_detection`, and all `reconcile_*`) are silently
dropped in production, so operators are blind to what the bot decides. Configure a
logging handler for rebar's loggers so these reach stdout/journald.

## Why
`src/rebar/review_bot/` contains NO logging setup (`grep` for `basicConfig`/`dictConfig`
in `src/rebar/` returns nothing). The container runs `uvicorn rebar.review_bot.app:app`;
uvicorn configures only its own `uvicorn.*` loggers and never the root or `rebar.*`
loggers. So a `logging.getLogger("rebar.review_bot.voter").info(...)` record has no
handler and falls through to Python's `logging.lastResort`, which emits **WARNING and
above only** — every INFO `_emit` line is discarded. This was directly observed during
incident triage: `journalctl` showed uvicorn's own access lines and the `print()`-to-stderr
markers (`VOTER_ERROR`, `ARTIFACT_EMIT_ERROR`) but ZERO `voter_voted`/`reconcile_*` lines,
making a healthy-but-slow reconciler look dead. The `_emit` docstrings call these
"observability markers" — dropping them violates that documented intent.

## What
Add a `_configure_logging()` to `src/rebar/review_bot/app.py`, called once at module import
(before `app = FastAPI(...)`), that attaches a `StreamHandler(sys.stdout)` to the `rebar`
logger at level `INFO` (overridable via a `REVIEW_BOT_LOG_LEVEL` env var), with a bare
`"%(message)s"` formatter (the `_emit` payload is already JSON), an idempotency marker on the
handler (so repeated imports/reloads don't stack handlers), and `propagate=False` (so records
do NOT double-log through uvicorn/root). Targeting the `rebar` logger specifically — not root
— avoids duplicating uvicorn's access logs.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/review_bot/app.py`: add `_configure_logging()` + `import sys`; call at import.
- `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py`: add a test asserting a `rebar.review_bot.*` INFO record
  reaches stdout after the app module configures logging (RED before the fix).
- Non-goal (separate follow-up): shipping logs to a shared service (CloudWatch Logs group).
  This change makes the logs *emitted*; centralizing them is tracked separately.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A `logging.getLogger("rebar.review_bot.voter").info(...)` record is written to stdout
      once the review-bot app module has configured logging; before the fix the same record
      produces no stdout output (proving the drop).
      Proving command: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_review_bot.py -k configure_logging -q`
      passes, and the test is confirmed RED on the pre-fix tree.
- [ ] The handler is idempotent: importing/configuring twice does not attach a second handler
      (no duplicate log lines).
      Proving command: assert `len([h for h in logging.getLogger("rebar").handlers if marker])
      == 1` after two `_configure_logging()` calls (in the test).
- [ ] `REVIEW_BOT_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG` lowers the effective level (env override honored); an
      absent/invalid value falls back to INFO.
      Proving command: covered by a parametrized assertion in the test.
- [ ] Existing gates stay green.
      Proving command: `make lint && make typecheck && python -m pytest tests/unit/test_review_bot.py -q` all exit 0.

file_impact.reason: RED test for rebar INFO reaching stdout

compiled_state.title: review-bot: configure logging so structured _emit INFO events reach stdout/journald
